task_completion.description: Create a fully-constrained C-channel cross-section sketch on the XY plane.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The FreeCAD Sketcher workbench shows a C-shaped profile consisting of two horizontal flanges connected by a vertical web, with fillet arcs at the four internal corners. Dimensional constraints should be visible or the constraint panel should show the sketch as fully constrained (zero degrees of freedom). The profile should clearly show the open-channel shape, not a closed rectangle.

task_completion.description: Create a quarter-circle arc sweep path on the XZ plane with 150 mm radius.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A quarter-circle arc is visible in a separate sketch, the XZ plane, or as a distinct path object in the Model tree. The arc should span 90 degrees. Look for a second Sketch object in the tree view distinct from the profile sketch.

task_completion.description: Sweep the C-channel profile along the arc path to create a curved solid bracket.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 3D curved bracket with a C-channel cross-section is visible in the 3D viewport. The shape should clearly show a channel section (open on one side) following a curved path, not a simple extruded bar or tube. The Tree View should show an AdditivePipe, Sweep, or Pipe feature that combines the profile sketch and the arc path.

task_completion.description: Add four 8 mm diameter mounting holes (two per end face, one in each flange) at both ends of the bracket.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Small circular holes are visible at both end faces of the bracket, within the flange regions of the C-channel cross-section. The Tree View should show Pocket or Hole features after the sweep operation. There should be 4 holes total (2 per end).

task_completion.description: Save as curved_bracket.FCStd and export as curved_bracket.step.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The FreeCAD window title bar shows 'curved_bracket' indicating the file has been saved. A Save As or Export dialog is visible showing the .step export, or a file manager or terminal confirms both curved_bracket.FCStd and curved_bracket.step exist in ~/Documents/FreeCAD/.

integrity.description: The agent used the FreeCAD GUI to model the bracket.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking FreeCAD tools, using workbenches (Part Design, Sketcher), drawing sketch geometry, and interacting with the 3D view, rather than just executing a Python script in the terminal.

integrity.description: The agent actually constructed the geometry step by step.
integrity.visual_evidence: The FreeCAD Tree View shows a construction history with multiple features (Sketches, AdditivePipe/Sweep, Pockets) rather than a single imported solid or mesh.

integrity.description: The agent used FreeCAD to save and export the files.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen using FreeCAD's File menu Save/Export options rather than creating dummy files via terminal commands.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires designing a curved structural bracket by sweeping a C-channel cross-section along a 90-degree arc. The C-channel profile has: 40 mm total height, 20 mm flange width, 3 mm web and flange thickness, 2 mm internal fillet radii. The sweep path is a quarter-circle arc with 150 mm centerline radius on the XZ plane. The resulting solid should be a visibly curved open-channel bracket, NOT a tube, solid bar, or closed box section. After sweeping, 4 mounting holes (8 mm diameter, 15 mm deep) should be present: 2 at each end face, one centered in each flange. The expected volume is approximately 50000 mm3. Output files: curved_bracket.FCStd and curved_bracket.step in ~/Documents/FreeCAD/.
